summary.intent: Fix a code review comment on PR #507 in the moltis repo by removing a redundant `is_chat_capable_model` filter in the provider-setup crate.
summary.outcome: Removed the redundant filter, updated the test comment to reflect that filtering happens upstream in `fetch_models_from_api`, verified tests pass and formatting is clean, then committed and pushed the fix.

repo: In the openai provider, `fetch_models_from_api` calls `parse_models_payload` which already filters for chat-capable models via `is_chat_capable_model`, so callers do not need to re-apply that filter.
PR reviews are done via GitHub (moltis-org/moltis) with Greptile performing automated code review comments.

code.finding: Had a redundant `is_chat_capable_model` filter that duplicated filtering already performed inside `fetch_models_from_api` → `parse_models_payload`. Removed the redundant filter and updated test comments to accurately describe where filtering occurs.
`parse_models_payload` is the authoritative location for chat-capable model filtering; `fetch_models_from_api` delegates to it, so results are always pre-filtered.

workflow: After pushing a fix for a PR review comment, resolve the conversation on GitHub to keep the PR clean.
Always run tests and formatting checks before committing review fixes.
